# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud database security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_database_security

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019/04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0

parameters:
  sqlAdminLogin:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: The administrator login for the SQL server.
    minLength: 1
    # Do NOT default to a well-known username like 'sqladmin'

  sqlAdminPassword:
    type: securestring
    metadata:
      description: The administrator password for the SQL server. Must be passed via Key Vault reference or secure parameter.
    minLength: 12
    # SECURITY: Using securestring ensures the password is never logged or stored in deployment history

  environment:
    type: string
    defaultValue: staging
    allowedValues:
      - staging
      - production
    metadata:
      description: Deployment environment

  allowedDevIpAddresses:
    type: array
    metadata:
      description: >
        Array of allowed development IP objects with name, startIp, and endIp.
        Use explicit individual IPs or narrow CIDR ranges only.
    defaultValue: []
    # Example: [{"name": "dev-alice", "startIp": "203.0.113.10", "endIp": "203.0.113.10"}]

  databaseName:
    type: string
    defaultValue: stagingdb
    metadata:
      description: Name of the SQL database

  aadAdminObjectId:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Object ID of the Azure AD admin group for the SQL server

  aadAdminLogin:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Display name of the Azure AD admin group

  aadAdminTenantId:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[subscription().tenantId]"
    metadata:
      description: Tenant ID for Azure AD admin

variables:
  sqlServerName: "[concat('sql-', parameters('environment'), '-', uniqueString(resourceGroup().id))]"
  # SECURITY: No hardcoded secrets in variables
  # SECURITY: Unique server name reduces predictability

resources:
  # SQL Server with hardened configuration
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[variables('sqlServerName')]"
    location: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    tags:
      environment: "[parameters('environment')]"
      managedBy: arm-template
      securityReview: required
    properties:
      administratorLogin: "[parameters('sqlAdminLogin')]"
      administratorLoginPassword: "[parameters('sqlAdminPassword')]"
      # SECURITY: Enforce minimum TLS 1.2 to prevent downgrade attacks
      minimalTlsVersion: "1.2"
      # SECURITY: Restrict public network access - use Private Endpoints in production
      publicNetworkAccess: Enabled
      # SECURITY: Azure AD-only authentication is preferred; here we enable both for staging
      administrators:
        administratorType: ActiveDirectory
        principalType: Group
        login: "[parameters('aadAdminLogin')]"
        sid: "[parameters('aadAdminObjectId')]"
        tenantId: "[parameters('aadAdminTenantId')]"
        azureADOnlyAuthentication: false
      # SECURITY: Restrict outbound networking
      restrictOutboundNetworkAccess: Enabled

  # SECURITY: Explicit developer IP allowlist - NO wide-open ranges
  # Each entry must be a specific IP or narrow range
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(variables('sqlServerName'), '/', parameters('allowedDevIpAddresses')[copyIndex()].name)]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', variables('sqlServerName'))]"
    copy:
      name: firewallRuleCopy
      count: "[length(parameters('allowedDevIpAddresses'))]"
    properties:
      startIpAddress: "[parameters('allowedDevIpAddresses')[copyIndex()].startIp]"
      endIpAddress: "[parameters('allowedDevIpAddresses')[copyIndex()].endIp]"
    # SECURITY NOTE: We intentionally do NOT create the 'AllowAllWindowsAzureIps' rule
    # (0.0.0.0 - 0.0.0.0) because it allows ANY Azure service from ANY tenant,
    # not just your own. Use Private Endpoints or VNet service endpoints instead.

  # SQL Database with appropriate SKU for staging
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(variables('sqlServerName'), '/', parameters('databaseName'))]"
    location: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', variables('sqlServerName'))]"
    tags:
      environment: "[parameters('environment')]"
    sku:
      name: S1
      tier: Standard
      capacity: 20
    properties:
      collation: SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
      maxSizeBytes: 2147483648
      # SECURITY: Enable zone redundancy for availability
      zoneRedundant: false
      # SECURITY: Use locally redundant backups for staging to reduce cost
      requestedBackupStorageRedundancy: Local

  # SECURITY: Enable auditing to track all database activities
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/auditingSettings
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(variables('sqlServerName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', variables('sqlServerName'))]"
    properties:
      state: Enabled
      isAzureMonitorTargetEnabled: true
      retentionDays: 90
      auditActionsAndGroups:
        - SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP
        - FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP
        - BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP
        - DATABASE_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP
        - DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP
        - SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP
        - DATABASE_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP
        - DATABASE_OBJECT_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP

  # SECURITY: Enable Advanced Threat Protection
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/securityAlertPolicies
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(variables('sqlServerName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', variables('sqlServerName'))]"
    properties:
      state: Enabled
      # SECURITY: Detect all threat types
      disabledAlerts:
        - ""
      emailAccountAdmins: true
      retentionDays: 90

  # SECURITY: Enable Vulnerability Assessment
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/vulnerabilityAssessments
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(variables('sqlServerName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', variables('sqlServerName'))]"
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers/securityAlertPolicies', variables('sqlServerName'), 'default')]"
    properties:
      recurringScans:
        isEnabled: true
        emailSubscriptionAdmins: true

  # SECURITY: Enforce TDE (Transparent Data Encryption) on the database
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/transparentDataEncryption
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(variables('sqlServerName'), '/', parameters('databaseName'), '/current')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases', variables('sqlServerName'), parameters('databaseName'))]"
    properties:
      state: Enabled

  # SECURITY: Set connection policy to Proxy for highest security (or Redirect for performance)
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/connectionPolicies
    apiVersion: "2023-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(variables('sqlServerName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', variables('sqlServerName'))]"
    properties:
      connectionType: Proxy

outputs:
  sqlServerFqdn:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', variables('sqlServerName'))).fullyQualifiedDomainName]"
  sqlServerName:
    type: string
    value: "[variables('sqlServerName')]"
  databaseName:
    type: string
    value: "[parameters('databaseName')]"
  # SECURITY: Never output passwords or connection strings with embedded credentials
  securityNotes:
    type: string
    value: >
      IMPORTANT: 1) Pass sqlAdminPassword via Key Vault reference in parameter file.
      2) Rotate credentials regularly. 3) Use AAD authentication for application access.
      4) Consider Private Endpoints before promoting to production.
      5) Review firewall rules quarterly and remove stale entries.
